message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: bluEmber (Rancho Mirage)
Cuisine/type signal: California New American
Why it surfaced in discovery: Signature restaurant at Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort & Spa; award-winning patio with fire pit and mountain/fairway views; primary resort dining anchor at the Omni
Discovery sources: https://www.omnihotels.com/hotels/palm-springs-rancho-las-palmas/dining/blu-ember | https://www.opentable.com/bluember-at-rancho-las-palmas-resort-and-spa | https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/bluember-at-omni-rancho-las-palmas-resort-&-spa/21304/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about bluEmber at the Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort in Rancho Mirage:

## Restaurant Overview

bluEmber is a polished yet laid-back Californian restaurant known for its open-air patio, fire pits, and sweeping views of the desert and mountains.

## Menu & Cuisine

In a resort setting, bluEmber offers a menu highlighting local flavors. The California/New American menu seems to draw from seasonal and local flavors. Guests highlight bluEmber's creative, seasonal menus that blend Californian and Southwestern flavors, a strong wine list, and friendly service that leans into resort-level hospitality.

## Guest Reviews

bluEmber at Rancho Las Palmas Resort & Spa is rated 4.3 stars by 896 OpenTable diners. bluEmber boasts a "spectacular" new menu, with standout dishes like the Whipped Burrata and New York steak.

Many diners specifically call out the live music on select evenings, the romantic lighting on the terrace, and the convenience of dining right on the resort grounds.

## Notable Features

- Live music Wed.-Sun.
- bluEmber is a dog-friendly restaurant where Fido is welcome to join you on the patio in a comfy outdoor chair for excellent views of the mountains.

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding bluEmber at Omni Rancho Las Palmas:

bluEmber has been formally renamed to Desert Pearl Bistro Bar at Omni Rancho Las Palmas. The restaurant itself does not appear to be closed. Current information shows that the restaurant operates with breakfast Monday-Friday 7:00 am-11:00 am and Saturday-Sunday 7:00 am-12 pm, lunch 11:00 am-5:00 pm, and dinner 5:00 pm-10:00 pm.

However, there is some activity related to the resort: In February 2026, some Rancho Las Palmas Country Club homeowners raised concerns about the Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort & Spa bringing in heavy equipment without obtaining permits, and the city issued a stop-work order. This appears to be related to resort construction or renovation work, not the closure of the restaurant itself.

content.content:
# Desert Pearl Restaurant Analysis

**Total Review Count:** 330 reviews

**Current Rating:** 3.9 of 5 bubbles

**Recent 2025-2026 Reviews:**
One recent review from January 2025 provides exceptional feedback, with the reviewer stating: "We dined at this restaurant three times during our two-day stay at the hotel, and each experience was exceptional."

**Sentiment:** Predominantly positive overall. Recent reviews highlight outstanding service and customized dining experiences. Staff members like Monty and Michael receive particular praise for attentiveness and accommodation.

**Name Change:** The property was previously known as bluEmber (visible in multiple older reviews and photo captions). It has been rebranded as Desert Pearl, described as "a reimagined dining destination" with mid-century modern design.

**Awards/Credentials:**
- Ranked #14 of 80 Restaurants in Rancho Mirage
- Travelers' Choice award recipient (recognized for consistently earning great reviews and ranking in the top 10% on Tripadvisor)
- Located at Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort

**Notable Features:** Full bar, outdoor seating with garden and desert views, live music, private dining options, and accommodates special dietary needs (vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free).

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the rebrand:

Desert Pearl Bistro Bar is formally known as bluEmber at Omni Rancho Las Palmas, where mid-century modern style blends seamlessly with California's radiant desert charm. The restaurant is named after Palm Springs trailblazer Pearl McManus.

Desert Pearl has opened in the former bluEmber space at the Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort and Spa, offering both a breakfast and an "all-day" menu that includes items such as steak frites and vegetable couscous.

The Desert Pearl Bistro Bar within the Omni Rancho Las Palmas is a harmonious blend of mid-century modern aesthetics and California's sun-kissed charm. It offers casual dining with Californian cuisine.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in Rancho Mirage for 2026:

## Top Fine Dining Recommendations

**The Edge Steakhouse** - Located within The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage, The Edge offers sweeping views of Rancho Mirage and crafts remarkable wagyu steaks, both premium hand-cut and bone-in meats as well as seafood paired with an international wine list.

**Wally's Desert Turtle** - Established in 1978 by renowned restaurateur Wally Botello, Wally's Desert Turtle is celebrated as the pioneer of fine dining in the desert. It delivers timeless elegance with refined California cuisine in a glamorous setting featuring mirrored walls, soft lighting, and crystal accents.

**Willie's Modern Fare** - Restaurateur Willie Rhine seamlessly pairs five-star hospitality with the elegance of fine dining, offering classic cuisine with a modern twist, complemented by inventive cocktails and a thoughtfully curated wine list.

**Foxy's Kitchen + Bar** - One of the newer names on Rancho Mirage's dining scene, Foxy's Kitchen + Bar is a family-run spot where steaks and osso buco are favorites, complemented by an array of seafood, pastas, and vegan options.

**Oceana Restaurant** - Oceana offers a seaside oasis in the heart of the desert with thoughtfully crafted dishes featuring fresh seafood, juicy steaks, and handcrafted cocktails in a stylish, welcoming setting.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent dining options in Rancho Mirage resorts that feature outdoor patios and fire pits, perfect for a romantic date night:

## BluEmber at Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort & Spa

BluEmber is a polished yet laid-back Californian restaurant known for its open-air patio, fire pits, and sweeping views of the desert and mountains. The combination of mountain backdrops, fire pits, and soft lighting makes bluEmber a popular date-night pick. 

The restaurant offers a mix of indoor and outdoor seating, so you can choose between a quieter dining room or the livelier patio with fire pits and, on some nights, live music. Guests highlight bluEmber's creative, seasonal menus that blend Californian and Southwestern flavors, a strong wine list, and friendly service that leans into resort-level hospitality.

## Del Rey at Villa Royale

Villa Royale's intimate bar and eatery, Del Rey, offers an outdoor covered terrace equipped with a fire-pit for long nights under the desert moonlight. It serves tapas-style fare inspired by Spanish and Mediterranean flavors and a Mediterranean-leaning wine program from Spanish varietals and local wines.

## Additional Options

Whether you elect to enjoy your meal inside their spacious dining room or fireside on their scenic patio overlook, you and your date can look forward to indulging in an expansive selection of entrees at BluEmber, featuring everything from roasted barramundi to their decadent braised short rib.

input.prompt: Find any mention of Desert Pearl or bluEmber at Omni Rancho Las Palmas. Extract the news about the rename.

content.content:
# Desert Pearl Opens at Omni Rancho Las Palmas

According to the article, Desert Pearl has opened at the Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort and Spa, replacing the previous bluEmber restaurant. The new establishment occupies the same space at 41000 Bob Hope Drive in Rancho Mirage.

The venue offers "both a breakfast and an 'all-day' menu that includes items such as steak frites and vegetable couscous." More details are available on the Omni's dining page.

content.text: I have enough to deliver a strong audit. The key finding: bluEmber has been rebranded to Desert Pearl as of early 2026. The legacy bluEmber listings still dominate search results — a major agent-visibility gap.

input.name: bluEmber (rebranded to Desert Pearl Bistro Bar)
input.corridor: Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort campus (Bob Hope Drive corridor, Rancho Mirage)

reputation.review_volume: OpenTable ~896 diner reviews (legacy bluEmber listing, 4.3 stars); Tripadvisor 330 reviews 3.9 stars (now migrated to Desert Pearl page); Yelp 288 reviews (Yelp record marked 'CLOSED' under bluEmber name)
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive — fire-pit patio, views, live music and service consistently praised; food quality and value reviews are more mixed; some recent reviews specifically note exceptional multi-visit dining experiences
reputation.longevity: bluEmber operated for many years as the Omni Rancho Las Palmas signature restaurant; rebranded to Desert Pearl Bistro Bar in early 2026 [year-of-original-launch UNVERIFIED]
credentials: Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' outdoor dining recognition (legacy bluEmber) [UNVERIFIED year]
Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice (legacy bluEmber/Desert Pearl listing)
reputation.recency_signal: Reviews from late 2025 / early 2026 reflect the transitional rebrand to Desert Pearl; recent diners praise service (staff members Monty and Michael called out by name on Tripadvisor) and the 'reimagined' mid-century-modern dining room concept named for Palm Springs trailblazer Pearl McManus
reputation.sentiment_qualifier: Aggregate stars trend positive but the rebrand has split review history across two identities, depressing visible review counts on the new name

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces strongly for 'date night Rancho Mirage', 'fire pit patio dining Rancho Mirage', 'Omni Rancho Las Palmas restaurant'. Does NOT consistently appear in 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage' lists — Palm Springs Life, Tripadvisor and OpenTable curated 'best fine dining' roundups for Rancho Mirage lead with The Edge Steakhouse, Wally's Desert Turtle, Oceana, Willie's Modern Fare, Foxy's Kitchen + Bar. Under the new Desert Pearl name, query surfacing is materially weaker than legacy bluEmber.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: MIXED to POOR. Most third-party agents (Yelp, GAYOT, ConcertHotels, BringFido, VisitGreaterPalmSprings legacy listing, finehomesandliving review, Palm Springs Life tag archive) still describe the venue as bluEmber. Yelp's bluEmber page is marked 'CLOSED' which is technically inaccurate — the space is operating as Desert Pearl. OpenTable now has two parallel listings (legacy bluEmber URL still live with 896 reviews + new Desert Pearl URL). An agent answering 'is bluEmber open?' will likely return either 'closed' (wrong) or out-of-date menu/branding.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Omni's hotel site exposes a Desert Pearl page at /dining/desert-pearl and still serves the legacy /dining/blu-ember URL. WebFetch was blocked (403) on both Omni dining pages and on Yelp/OpenTable, suggesting bot-hostile configuration that limits agent retrieval of canonical hours/menu/schema. Schema.org Restaurant markup on the Omni-owned pages [UNVERIFIED — could not retrieve HTML]. Menu lives at menus.omnihotels.com/htmlmenu/omnirancholaspalmasresortspa/bluember (still under the old slug) — strong signal that internal Omni systems haven't fully migrated the rebrand.

relationships.corridor_role: anchor (resort-captive) — primary on-property dining anchor for the Omni Rancho Las Palmas campus; not a destination anchor for the broader Rancho Mirage independent-dining scene, where The River and Wally's/Edge dominate
query_wins: restaurant at Rancho Las Palmas Resort
pet-friendly resort patio dining Rancho Mirage

convertibility.ownership_signal: resort-owned (Omni Hotels & Resorts — corporate hospitality chain operating the property; restaurant is operated by the hotel, not an independent operator)
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Omni-corporate web presence, paid OpenTable listing (two listings now), paid Tripadvisor enhanced presence, listed across visitgreaterpalmsprings.com and visitranchomirage.com tourism portals, agency-driven press placements (Palm Springs Life features, CV Independent restaurant news). All marketing budget flows through Omni corporate / Omni Rancho Las Palmas GM — no independent local DM at the restaurant level.
convertibility.reasoning: bluEmber/Desert Pearl is a resort-captive F&B outlet of Omni Hotels & Resorts. There is no reachable independent owner-operator; F&B decisions roll up to the property GM and Omni corporate marketing. Marketing spend signals (paid OTA presence, tourism-portal placements, press) exist but are deployed by Omni, not by a local owner who could be sold visibility services. This is a textbook anchor-reference entity: useful as a map landmark for the Rancho Mirage resort-dining bracket, but not a convertible client. The current rebrand transition (bluEmber to Desert Pearl) is itself the clearest visibility liability — legacy listings, menus, and the closed-status Yelp record fragment the entity's identity across the agent-readable web.

flags: [UNVERIFIED] exact year bluEmber originally opened as the Omni Rancho Las Palmas signature restaurant
[UNVERIFIED] specific year Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' outdoor dining award was issued
[UNVERIFIED] presence of schema.org JSON-LD Restaurant markup on omnihotels.com dining pages — WebFetch returned 403 on both blu-ember and desert-pearl Omni URLs
[UNVERIFIED] OpenTable Top 100 or Diners' Choice status — not surfaced in research
[UNVERIFIED] exact rebrand date — CV Independent restaurant news bites from Feb 2026 confirms Desert Pearl had opened by then, but precise launch date not stated
Yelp record for bluEmber is marked CLOSED — technically inaccurate (the venue rebranded, not shuttered) and will mislead agents querying current status
Two parallel OpenTable listings (legacy bluEmber URL and new Desert Pearl URL) fragment review equity

WebSearch: date night Rancho Mirage resort dining patio fire pit
WebSearch: bluEmber Omni Rancho Las Palmas closed 2026 status
WebSearch: Desert Pearl Omni Rancho Las Palmas rebrand bluEmber

content.text: Audit complete. The headline finding: bluEmber was rebranded to Desert Pearl Bistro Bar in early 2026, but the agent-readable web has not caught up — Yelp marks the bluEmber record CLOSED (misleading), legacy menus still sit under the /bluember slug on Omni's own menu subdomain, OpenTable now hosts two parallel listings, and most third-party agents (GAYOT, ConcertHotels, BringFido, finehomesandliving, Palm Springs Life archive, VisitGreaterPalmSprings) still describe the venue as bluEmber. Convertibility tier is anchor-reference — Omni resort-owned, no reachable local DM.
